sections.paragraph:
ἐκδιηγέομαι fut. ἐκδιηγήσομαι; aor. inf. ἐκδιηγήσασθαι (s. διηγέομαι; Hippocr., Progn. II p. 110; Aristot., Rhet. 23 p. 1434b, 4; Galen; LXX; ParJer 4:12; Philo, Mos. 1, 235; Jos., Bell. 5, 567, Ant. 5, 279; Just., D. 22:9 [for διηγῇ Ps 49, 16 w. cod. A]) to provide detailed information when telling someth., tell (in detail) τὶ someth. Ac 15:3. Abs. w. dat. of pers. 13:41 (Hab 1:5); 1 Cl 35:7 v.l. (for διηγέομαι).—S. DELG s.v. ἡγέομαι.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

inf. inf. = infinitive

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

someth. someth. = something

dat. dat. = dative

pers. pers. = person(s)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 300
